projects.overview: The hotel website currently lacks the necessary detail and structure for AI assistants to recommend it effectively. It is particularly weak in providing specific information about rooms, dining, and location, which are crucial for guest decision-making. The most important step is to add dedicated FAQ sections and Quick Facts across key pages to address common guest inquiries.

projects.title: Add FAQ Sections Across Key Pages
projects.why: FAQs help AI understand and answer common guest questions, making the hotel more likely to be recommended for specific needs.
steps: Add a FAQ section to the Homepage to address general inquiries.
Include FAQs on the Rooms page to cover occupancy, views, and ideal guest types.
Create FAQs for the Dining page to explain dining options and benefits.
Add a FAQ section to the Location page to detail transport options.
Include FAQs on the Meetings & Events page to outline capacities and catering options.
Add FAQs to the Luxury/About page to highlight unique features and recognition.
Ensure the Offers and Experiences pages have FAQs to clarify what makes them special.

projects.title: Implement Quick Facts Blocks
projects.why: Quick Facts provide concise, essential information that AI can easily retrieve and quote, improving the hotel's visibility in AI recommendations.
steps: Add Quick Facts to the Homepage summarizing key hotel features.
Include Quick Facts on the Offers page to highlight special deals.
Create Quick Facts for the Experiences page to showcase unique activities.

projects.title: Add AI Summaries to Key Pages
projects.why: AI summaries help AI assistants quickly understand the page content, improving the chances of the hotel being recommended for relevant searches.
steps: Write a concise AI summary for the Homepage.
Include an AI summary on the Offers page.
Add an AI summary to the Experiences page.

projects.title: Enhance Room Details
projects.why: Detailed room information helps AI match guest preferences with the right accommodations, increasing the likelihood of a recommendation.
steps: Provide detailed occupancy information for each room type.
Describe the views available from each room.
Clarify who each room type is ideal for (e.g., families, couples).
Add comparisons between room types to help guests choose.

projects.title: Improve Dining Page Content
projects.why: Clear dining information helps AI recommend the hotel to guests looking for specific dining experiences.
steps: Explain who the dining options are ideal for (e.g., romantic dinners, family meals).
Highlight unique dining features and reasons to choose the hotel's dining options.

projects.title: Add Trust Signals with Schema Markup
projects.why: Trust signals like reviews and awards help AI see the hotel as credible and reliable, which can boost recommendations.
steps: Implement Review and AggregateRating schema on relevant pages.
Add any available awards or recognitions to the Luxury/About page.
Ensure all pages use appropriate schema markup for better AI visibility.

actionPlan.intro: Based on the pages crawled and the recommendation-readiness analysis, these are the highest-impact actions that would most improve AI recommendation confidence. Actions that unlock searches the site cannot currently answer are listed first.

topPriorities.title: Strengthen your Rooms page
topPriorities.why: This page exists but is missing key elements AI looks for, limiting how confidently it can recommend you.
topPriorities.outcome: Adding Occupancy, View info, Who it’s for would let AI fully understand and recommend this page's strengths.

topPriorities.title: Strengthen your Meetings & Events page
topPriorities.why: This page exists but is missing key elements AI looks for, limiting how confidently it can recommend you.
topPriorities.outcome: Adding Capacities, Catering, FAQ would let AI fully understand and recommend this page's strengths.

topPriorities.title: Strengthen your Offers page
topPriorities.why: This page exists but is missing key elements AI looks for, limiting how confidently it can recommend you.
topPriorities.outcome: Adding Clear positioning, Quick Facts block, AI summary would let AI fully understand and recommend this page's strengths.

topPriorities.title: Strengthen your Experiences page
topPriorities.why: This page exists but is missing key elements AI looks for, limiting how confidently it can recommend you.
topPriorities.outcome: Adding Clear positioning, Quick Facts block, AI summary would let AI fully understand and recommend this page's strengths.

topPriorities.title: Strengthen your Homepage page
topPriorities.why: This page exists but is missing key elements AI looks for, limiting how confidently it can recommend you.
topPriorities.outcome: Adding Quick Facts block, AI summary, FAQ section would let AI fully understand and recommend this page's strengths.

topPriorities.title: Strengthen your Suites page
topPriorities.why: This page exists but is missing key elements AI looks for, limiting how confidently it can recommend you.
topPriorities.outcome: Adding Who it’s for, Ideal-For, Comparison would let AI fully understand and recommend this page's strengths.

topPriorities.title: Strengthen your Dining page
topPriorities.why: This page exists but is missing key elements AI looks for, limiting how confidently it can recommend you.
topPriorities.outcome: Adding Who it’s for, Why choose it, FAQ would let AI fully understand and recommend this page's strengths.

topPriorities.title: Strengthen your Location page
topPriorities.why: This page exists but is missing key elements AI looks for, limiting how confidently it can recommend you.
topPriorities.outcome: Adding Transport options, FAQ would let AI fully understand and recommend this page's strengths.

topPriorities.title: Strengthen your Luxury / About page
topPriorities.why: This page exists but is missing key elements AI looks for, limiting how confidently it can recommend you.
topPriorities.outcome: Adding Awards / recognition, FAQ would let AI fully understand and recommend this page's strengths.

quickWins.action: Add Quick Facts block to your Homepage page
quickWins.why: Gives AI a scannable list of hard facts (parking, spa, transfer, pets) it can quote directly instead of guessing.

quickWins.action: Add AI summary to your Homepage page
quickWins.why: A 1–2 sentence positioning line AI can lift verbatim when asked who the hotel is best for.

quickWins.action: Add FAQ section to your Homepage page
quickWins.why: Q&A pairs mirror how guests phrase questions, making your answers easy for AI to match and quote.

quickWins.action: Add Comparison to your Rooms page
quickWins.why: Comparative detail is what lets AI rank you above alternatives instead of leaving you at a tentative maybe.

quickWins.action: Add FAQ to your Rooms page
quickWins.why: Q&A pairs mirror how guests phrase questions, making your answers easy for AI to match and quote.

quickWins.action: Add Comparison to your Suites page
quickWins.why: Comparative detail is what lets AI rank you above alternatives instead of leaving you at a tentative maybe.

quickWins.action: Add FAQ to your Suites page
quickWins.why: Q&A pairs mirror how guests phrase questions, making your answers easy for AI to match and quote.

quickWins.action: Add FAQ to your Dining page
quickWins.why: Q&A pairs mirror how guests phrase questions, making your answers easy for AI to match and quote.

whatNotToDo.message: Do not try to create or rewrite everything at once. Start with the items below — they unlock the largest number of searches the site currently cannot answer. Once those are live, work down the priority list.

existing.label: Offers — /offers
faqs: What makes this hotel distinctive?
Who is this hotel best suited to?
Where exactly is the hotel located?
What is included in a stay?

existing.label: Experiences — /offer/the-kinky-boots-walk-this-way-experience
faqs: What makes this hotel distinctive?
Who is this hotel best suited to?
Where exactly is the hotel located?
What is included in a stay?

existing.label: Rooms — /accommodation
faqs: What room types are available?
How many guests does each room sleep?
Which rooms have the best views?
Which room is best for families / couples?

existing.label: Rooms — /accommodation/superior-room
faqs: What room types are available?
How many guests does each room sleep?
Which rooms have the best views?
Which room is best for families / couples?

existing.label: Meetings & Events — /meetings-events
faqs: What meeting and event spaces are available?
What is the capacity of each room?
Is catering available?
How far is the airport?

existing.label: Rooms — /accommodation/deluxe-room
faqs: What room types are available?
How many guests does each room sleep?
Which rooms have the best views?
Which room is best for families / couples?

existing.label: Homepage — /
faqs: What makes this hotel distinctive?
Who is this hotel best suited to?
Where exactly is the hotel located?

faqs: What restaurants are at the hotel?

existing.label: Location — /location
faqs: Where is the hotel located?
How far is the airport?
What attractions are nearby?
How do I get there?

categories.label: FAQ Coverage
categories.evidence: No FAQ section is present in the provided text.
categories.comment: The website lacks a dedicated FAQ section, which limits its ability to answer common guest questions in a clear format. Adding a FAQ section would improve AI citation potential.

categories.label: Specificity
categories.evidence: Superior Room Queen Size Bed Avg 21 m² / 226 SQF 2 guests
categories.comment: The site provides specific details about room sizes and capacities, which is useful for AI. However, more specific information about amenities and services would enhance specificity.

categories.label: Quotable Phrasing
categories.evidence: A theatrical dining experience awaits you at L’oscar Restaurant, featuring an illuminated Onyx bar with mirrored ceilings and stunning original art.
categories.comment: The text includes some factual elements but is often embedded in flowery language. More concise, factual statements would be more quotable for AI.

categories.label: Summary / Quick-Facts Block
categories.evidence: No summary or quick-facts block is present in the provided text.
categories.comment: The website lacks a concise summary or quick-facts block. Adding one would help AI quickly retrieve key information about the hotel.

categories.label: Clarity & Assertiveness
categories.evidence: L’oscar is a turn-of-the-century architectural gem, created from a Baroque-style, Grade II listed former church.
categories.comment: The text provides some clear, verifiable information but often mixes it with subjective language. More assertive, fact-based statements would improve clarity.

categories.label: Semantic Variety
categories.evidence: Boutique Hotel London | Luxury Hotels London | L'oscar London
categories.comment: The text frequently repeats similar phrases about luxury and boutique status without adding new information. More varied descriptions would enhance semantic variety.

categories.label: Concrete Language
categories.comment: The text uses marketing metaphors and buzzwords, which can obscure concrete information. Using plain, descriptive language would make the content more accessible to AI.

layers.definition: Whether core hotel facts (parking, breakfast, pets, accessibility, airport, check-in, cancellation) have a single, consistent source of truth.
layers.why: AI assistants quote facts directly. When facts are scattered or missing, AI either cannot answer or risks giving a wrong answer — both lose the recommendation.
evidence: Check-in / out: Across 11 pages, no dedicated page.
Cancellation: Across 11 pages, no dedicated page.
layers.scoreReason: Parking, Pets, Airport transfer could not be found at all; 2 fact(s) are scattered across pages with no single dedicated source. AI has to guess instead of quoting one reliable answer.
layers.fix: Create a dedicated Parking page, a dedicated Pets page, a dedicated Airport transfer page.

layers.definition: Whether the expected core pages exist: Homepage, Rooms, Dining, Spa, Location, Offers, Meetings, Contact.
layers.why: These are the pages AI crawlers expect on a hotel site. Missing core pages create blind spots the AI fills with nothing — or with a competitor.
layers.scoreReason: 7 of 8 expected core pages were found. Missing: Spa.
layers.fix: Create a Spa page.

layers.definition: Whether pages exist that target recommendation intent (family hotel, romantic, business, spa, pet-friendly, accessible, parking, near-airport, lake views).
layers.why: AI matches a guest’s intent ("best family hotel in Geneva") to pages built around that intent. No intent page = no match.
layers.scoreReason: Only 0 of 8 guest-intent pages exist. Without a page built around an intent, AI has nothing to match a search like "best family hotel" to.
layers.fix: Create a Family page, a Romantic page, a Business page, a Spa page.

layers.definition: Whether dedicated fact pages exist for Parking, Breakfast, Pets, Accessibility, Airport Transfer, Check-in/out, Cancellation, and a central FAQ.
layers.why: A dedicated, crawlable page per fact is the most reliable way for AI to retrieve and trust a specific answer.
layers.scoreReason: 0 of 6 dedicated fact pages were found. Missing: Parking, Breakfast, Pets, Accessibility, Airport transfer, Central FAQ. A dedicated, crawlable page per fact is the most reliable way for AI to retrieve a specific answer.
layers.fix: Create a dedicated Parking page, a dedicated Breakfast page, a dedicated Pets page, a dedicated Accessibility page.

layers.definition: For each room page: does it explain what the room is, who it’s for, why choose it, and when to book — with Overview, Quick Facts, Occupancy, View, Ideal-For, Comparison, FAQ.
layers.why: Rich room data lets AI recommend a specific room for a specific guest ("best room for a couple"), not just the hotel in general.
layers.scoreReason: Room pages describe the rooms but average only 25% of what AI needs. Missing across them: Occupancy, View info, Who it’s for, Ideal-For, Comparison, FAQ. Without who-it's-for and comparison detail, AI can recommend the hotel but not the right room.
layers.fix: Add Occupancy, View info, Who it’s for, Ideal-For. to your room pages.

layers.definition: Whether major pages contain a Quick Facts block and a concise AI Summary paragraph.
layers.why: These structured blocks are the easiest thing for AI to lift verbatim, making your answers more likely to be quoted accurately.
missing: Quick Facts block on key pages
layers.scoreReason: 0 key page(s) have a Quick Facts block and 0 have an AI summary, out of 4 checked. These structured blocks are the easiest thing for AI to lift verbatim, so when they're absent your answers are less likely to be quoted accurately.
layers.fix: Add Quick Facts block on key pages, AI summary on key pages.

layers.definition: Whether major pages answer WHAT, WHO, HOW, WHY, COMPARISON and FAQ for their topic.
layers.why: AI answers guest questions by matching this structure. Pages that only describe, without answering, are weak retrieval sources.
layers.scoreReason: Your pages cover 1 of 5 question types AI looks for (WHAT). Missing: WHO, WHY, COMPARISON, FAQ. Pages that only describe — without answering WHO/WHY/comparison/FAQ — are weak sources for AI.
layers.fix: Make sure pages answer WHO, WHY, COMPARISON, FAQ for their topic.

layers.definition: Whether pages name real entities — landmarks, airports, stations, neighbourhoods, attractions.
layers.why: Named entities anchor the hotel in a location graph AI understands, improving location-based recommendations.
layers.scoreReason: 171 named places/landmarks were detected across the crawled pages, which is enough to anchor the hotel in a location AI understands.
layers.fix: Nothing missing.

layers.definition: Whether pages explicitly help AI recommend (best room for couples / families / business; comparison and ideal-for sections).
layers.why: Explicit recommendation cues let AI confidently say "this hotel is best for X" instead of hedging.
layers.scoreReason: No comparison or "best for" sections were found, so AI has nothing explicit to base a recommendation on and tends to hedge.
layers.fix: Add comparison and best-for sections to room and intent pages.

layers.definition: Whether local-knowledge pages exist (things to do, family guide, business guide, romantic guide).
layers.why: Local guides signal authority and answer the planning questions guests ask AI alongside the booking.
missing: Local guide pages (things to do, area guide)
layers.scoreReason: 0 local-guide page(s) were found. Local guides (things to do, area guides) signal authority and answer the planning questions guests ask AI alongside booking.
layers.fix: Add local guide pages: things to do, family guide, romantic guide.

layers.definition: Whether reviews, awards, ratings and recognition are present and machine-readable (review schema).
layers.why: AI weighs trust signals heavily when choosing which hotel to surface first among similar options.
layers.scoreReason: No trust signals detected. Without machine-readable review/rating data, AI cannot read your reputation, which it weighs heavily when choosing which hotel to surface first.
layers.fix: Add Review and AggregateRating schema so AI can read your ratings.

layers.definition: Whether pages form topic clusters (e.g. Family page → Family Rooms → Family Offers → Family FAQ).
layers.why: Strong internal linking helps crawlers discover all your pages and understand which pages belong to which intent.
layers.scoreReason: 2 topic cluster(s) with strong interlinking were detected. When related pages don’t link to each other, crawlers may miss pages and can’t tell which belong to which intent.
layers.fix: Link each intent hub to its related pages (e.g. Family → Family Rooms → Family Offers → Family FAQ).

layers.definition: Whether structured data is present: Hotel, HotelRoom, FAQPage, Review, AggregateRating, Restaurant, Offer, Breadcrumb.
layers.why: Schema is the most direct, unambiguous way to feed facts to AI. It removes guesswork from retrieval.
layers.scoreReason: 4 of 9 structured-data types were found (Hotel, HotelRoom, Offer, BreadcrumbList). Missing: FAQPage, Review, AggregateRating, Restaurant, Event. Schema is the most direct way to feed facts to AI without guesswork.
layers.fix: Add FAQPage schema, Review schema, AggregateRating schema, Restaurant schema.

layers.definition: How many of the important guest questions the website can actually answer, based on the prompt analysis.
layers.why: The breadth of answerable questions is a direct measure of how often AI can use your site to respond to a guest.
layers.scoreReason: The site can confidently or partly answer 0 of 0 tracked guest questions. The breadth of answerable questions is a direct measure of how often AI can use your site to respond to a guest.
layers.fix: Add FAQs and fact pages for the unanswered questions listed in Pillar 2.

trust.why: Trust signals (reviews, awards, ratings, recognition) are weighed heavily by AI when choosing which hotel to surface first.

importantPages.label: Homepage
examples:
Example format (fill with your own facts):
Parking: Yes · Spa: Yes · Lake view: Some rooms · Airport transfer: On request · Pets: Allowed
Example format (write your own 1–2 sentences): "[Hotel] is a [type] hotel in [area], best suited to [guest types], known for [1–2 distinctive features]."
Example questions to answer: Does the hotel have parking? · Is breakfast included? · How far is the airport? · Is the spa open to non-residents?

architecture.note: Architecture layers are objective findings computed from crawled pages only.
